BreathingCrisisText.html.source: In a breathing crisis, the paralyzed arytenoid cartilages block the airway and cut off airflow to the lungs. In extreme cases, the crisis escalates into a breathing emergency that requires care at a veterinary facility to restore breathing. These questions are about breathing emergencies that required care from a licensed veterinarian.

BreathingCrisisDidHave.title.source: Did {CmpnName} ever have a breathing crisis that required an emergency visit to a veterinary professional?

BreathingCrisisCount.title.source: How many times did {CmpnName} visit a veterinary professional due to an LP/GOLPP-associated breathing crisis?

BreathingCrisisCost.title.source: About what was the total pre-insurance cost of these visits?

BreathingCrisisCost.choices.DONT_KNOW.te.source: Don't know or prefer not to say

BreathingCrisisAdvice.title.source: Did a veterinary professional give you any advice on how to deal with an LP-induced breathing emergency/crisis?

BreathingCrisisAdviceType.title.source: What advice did they give you to help you deal with a breathing crisis?

BreathingCrisisAdviceType.choices.COOL_P.source: Get them to a cool place

BreathingCrisisAdviceType.choices.COOLIN.source: Apply ice, a cooling vest, or some other cooling agent

BreathingCrisisAdviceType.choices.EMERGE.source: Bring them to an emergency facility immediately

BreathingCrisisProvidedSubstance.title.source: Did a veterinary professional provide you with a substance or product to help deal with an LP/GOLPP-induced breathing emergency/crisis?

BreathingCrisisProvidedSubstanceType.tit.source: What substance or product did they provide?

BreathingCrisisSubstanceForm.title.source: In what form was the sedative or steroid provided?
